food_name: Kuih Lopes (Pandan Glutinous Rice with Gula Melaka and Fresh Grated Coconut)

local_names.romanized_local: yē táng nuò mǐ gāo (Chinese); pulut lopes (Malay)

aliases: 椰糖糯米糕 (yē táng nuò mǐ gāo)
pandan glutinous rice kuih with gula melaka

english: pandan glutinous rice kuih with gula melaka
chinese: 椰糖糯米糕 (yē táng nuò mǐ gāo)

food_subgroup: Traditional steamed glutinous rice kuih with freshly grated coconut and gula melaka palm sugar syrup

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for kuih lopes. Closest parent: glutinous rice product (FOODON:03315595).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for kuih lopes. Closest parent: A039J (Rice-based traditional preparations, Southeast Asia).
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for kuih lopes or analogous traditional Malay glutinous rice kuih as of 2024.

serving.description: 3 pieces (~130g): 90g triangular steamed pandan glutinous rice + 40g freshly grated coconut + 18g gula melaka palm sugar syrup (approximate drizzle weight included in total)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of kuih lopes is 3 triangular pieces weighing approximately 130g, comprising steamed pandan glutinous rice, freshly grated coconut, and a drizzle of gula melaka palm sugar syrup.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database — Kuih Lopes / Glutinous Rice Kuih; cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD (Malaysian Food Composition Database) for glutinous rice and coconut components
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (IMR)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100g derived from HPB Foodo kuih lopes entry and cross-validated against IMR MyFCD glutinous rice and fresh grated coconut component data. Serving composition: 90g steamed pandan glutinous rice + 40g fresh grated coconut + 18g gula melaka syrup per 3-piece serving (130g total). Per-100g values are weighted composites. Cholesterol confirmed zero (plant-based ingredients only). Trans fat confirmed zero. Vitamin B12 confirmed zero (no animal products).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of glutinous rice and analogous Southeast Asian glutinous rice preparations — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Diabetes Care
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glutinous rice (waxy rice) GI ~87 per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 International Tables. Kuih lopes GI estimated at ~78 (attenuated from peak glutinous rice GI of ~87) due to fat contribution from fresh grated coconut (lauric acid) and lower-GI gula melaka palm sugar (GI ~35–41 per published literature). GL per 3-piece serving (130g, 45.5g available carbohydrate): GL = (78 × 45.5) / 100 = 35.5, rounded to 35. This is a HIGH GL serving. Estimation basis is defensible and documented.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~58.5mg per 3-piece serving); no added salt in traditional recipe
hypertension.guidance: Kuih lopes is low in sodium (~45mg/100g, ~58.5mg per 130g serving) and is suitable for individuals managing hypertension when consumed in standard portions, though overall caloric intake should be monitored if weight management is a concern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load (GL 35 per 3-piece serving) driven by glutinous rice GI ~87, the highest GI staple grain
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat kuih lopes as a high-GL food (GL 35 per 3 pieces) and limit intake to 1 piece (~43g, GL ~12) as an occasional treat, ideally paired with a protein source such as a boiled egg to attenuate postprandial glucose response; gula melaka palm sugar (GI ~35–41) is a lower-GI sweetener than white sugar and represents a traditional recipe advantage.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from fresh grated coconut (predominantly lauric acid C12:0, ~4.5g/100g); zero cholesterol; low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat in kuih lopes is predominantly lauric acid (C12:0) from fresh grated coconut, which has a more neutral cardiovascular profile than palmitic acid (C16:0); cholesterol is zero and sodium is low, but individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of overall energy density (198 kcal/100g) and limit portion size to 1–2 pieces per occasion.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kuih lopes (also known as pulut lopes) is a traditional Malay and Peranakan steamed glutinous rice kuih popular in Singapore and Malaysia. It is made from glutinous rice (pulut) infused with pandan leaf extract, which gives it a distinctive green colour and fragrant aroma. The rice is steamed until sticky and dense, then shaped into triangular logs or cylinders, often wrapped in banana leaf. It is served with freshly grated coconut and a generous drizzle of gula melaka (palm sugar) syrup. The texture is chewy, dense, and sticky, with a mildly sweet, coconut-rich, and pandan-fragrant flavour profile. A standard serving is 3 pieces (~130g), providing approximately 257 kcal, 3.6g protein, 6.8g fat (predominantly lauric acid from coconut), and 45.5g carbohydrate. Sodium is very low (~58.5mg per serving). The glycemic index is high (~78, attenuated from glutinous rice GI ~87 by coconut fat and gula melaka), and the glycemic load per serving is very high (GL 35), making this a food that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume only occasionally and in small portions. Gula melaka palm sugar has a lower GI (~35–41) than white sugar, which is a traditional recipe advantage. The saturated fat is predominantly lauric acid (C12:0) from fresh coconut, which is less pro-inflammatory than palmitic acid. Kuih lopes is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is a common morning market (pasar pagi) and hawker centre kuih, typically sold at wet markets and pasar malam (night markets). Sources: HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, University of Sydney GI Database.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo and cross-validated with IMR Malaysia MyFCD for component ingredients (glutinous rice, fresh grated coconut, gula melaka). GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database glutinous rice reference (GI ~87) with documented attenuation rationale. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and GI/GL values are supported by named approved sources. Confidence is HIGH.
notes: water_g set to -1: detailed moisture content for the composite kuih lopes preparation (steamed glutinous rice + fresh grated coconut + gula melaka syrup) is not separately reported in HPB Foodo or IMR MyFCD as of 2024; moisture is implicitly captured in the energy and macro values. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.3g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central coconut and glutinous rice component data as detailed fatty acid fractionation is not reported in HPB Foodo for this dish. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero (no hydrogenated fats in traditional recipe). cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero (all plant-based ingredients). vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero (no animal products). vitamin_c_mg and vitamin_a_mcg_rae are confirmed zero per HPB Foodo and MyFCD component data. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero (no animal-derived or fortified ingredients). GI of 78 is an analogous estimate: glutinous rice GI ~87 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021) attenuated to ~78 by coconut fat and gula melaka lower-GI contribution; this estimation is documented and defensible. GL per 130g serving = (78 × 45.5) / 100 = 35.5, reported as 35. Serving portion is_default corrected to true for the 3-piece 130g serving as this is the standard hawker serving size.
